business.headline: Dalal Street adds Rs 17 lakh crore as Iran-US ceasefire boosts marketsInvestor wealth surged by around Rs 17.1 lakh crore in a single session, taking total BSE market capitalisation to Rs 445.8 lakh crore. The S&P BSE Sensex rallied 2,946.32 points to end at 77,562.90, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 873.35 points to close at 23,997.35.

Resilient, safe, strong: RBI assures banking system safe amid recent jittersRBI Governor highlighted that the broader banking system remains stable despite global challenges. He said there are no systemic risks to the profitability or health of banks, even as global uncertainty remains due to the West Asia conflict.

Why did RBI keep repo rate unchanged at 5.25%? Know these 5 reasonsNo change in the repo rate this time, but the move is far from random. The RBI has carefully weighed multiple factors before deciding to hold at 5.25%. Here are five reasons behind the decision.

Sensex jumps 3,000 points: What does this mean for stock market investors?The bulls on Dalal Street are back in control for now, but whether they can hold ground will depend on oil, flows and earnings in the days ahead.

Bitcoin above $70,000, altcoins surge up to 23%: Is a new crypto rally starting?The crypto market is showing fresh signs of strength. Bitcoin has reclaimed the $70,000 level, and altcoins are posting strong gains, leaving investors wondering if this momentum can sustain in the coming weeks.

RBI's subtle growth warning is easy to miss, but it's thereRBI's growth warning was subtle, but it was evident in Governor Sanjay Malhotra's monetary policy statement. In this article, we take a closer look at the biggest risks to India's economy.

Sensex, Nifty jump, but IT stocks trade flat. Will they perform post Q4 results?The market rally was broad-based, with strong gains across banking, financial, auto and other sectors. However, IT stocks stood out as the only laggards, trading flat or with marginal gains despite the sharp market rise.

Gold up Rs 5,000, silver jumps Rs 14,000: Will the rally continue?Precious metals are glittering again. In a short span, gold prices have risen by Rs 5,000 and silver has surged Rs 14,000, prompting fresh interest from investors trying to decode whether this momentum will continue.

What is RBI's plan after rupee's recent fall vs US dollar? RBI Governor explainsRBI's focus will remain on managing sharp swings in the currency rather than controlling its direction, as global factors continue to drive movements in the rupee.

RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%: How it impacts your EMI and home loan plansIn a welcome move for homebuyers, the RBI has kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25%. This means no immediate increase in EMIs, offering much-needed relief and stability to borrowers planning their finances.

Elevated energy prices, disruptions at Hormuz likely to impact growth: RBI GuvThe warning comes even as the central bank projects India's economy to grow 6.9% this financial year, signalling that while the growth engine is intact, fresh global shocks are building.

Gold at Rs 1.53 lakh, silver jumps 5%: Why are prices rising today?The rise in precious metals comes alongside a broader rally in equities and a sharp fall in crude oil prices, as investors adjust to changing risk conditions.

RBI keeps key lending rates unchanged at 5.25%, stance remains 'neutral'The Reserve Bank of India has kept its key policy rate unchanged at 5.25%, marking no change since December 2025, as policymakers chose to wait amid rising global uncertainty linked to the West Asia conflict.

Adani moves US court seeking dismissal of SEC fraud case, says lawsuit lacks jurisdictionIn a pre-motion letter filed ahead of a planned April 30 motion seeking dismissal of the lawsuit, Adanis through their lawyers said the SEC's claims over a 2021 bond sale by the group's renewable energy arm, Adani Green Energy Ltd (AGEL) are legally flawed on multiple grounds.
IndiGo shares rally 10%: Why is the stock up today?As of 9:50 am, IndiGo shares were trading at Rs 4,681.50, up 9.68% on the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE).

Iran-US announce ceasefire: How will it impact RBI MPC decision today?RBI MPC: The ceasefire between the US and Iran, which has led to a sharp fall in crude oil prices, is expected to play a key role in shaping the RBI's stance. Lower oil prices ease pressure on inflation and the overall economy, giving the central bank more room to maintain stability.

Sensex surges nearly 3,000 points: 4 things investors should knowAs of 11:02 am, the BSE Sensex surged nearly 3,000 points to 77,535.53, while the Nifty 50 neared the 24,000 mark. Investor wealth jumped by over Rs 14 lakh crore in early trade.

Wave of green on Dalal Street as markets cheer Iran-US ceasefire, Hormuz openingDalal Street surged as ceasefire hopes and falling crude oil prices boosted investor confidence, adding Rs 14 lakh crore in early trade.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Stock market likely to rally today. Here's whyStock markets are likely to open higher today as crude oil falls below $100 and global markets rally. Here's what is driving Sensex and Nifty today.

What caused crude oil prices to drop below $100 today as Eastern markets jump?Brent crude dropped to around $94–95 per barrel, while US WTI crude fell to about $96, marking a steep fall of over 13% in a single session.

entertainment.headline: Allu Arjun's 44th birthday draws crowds and fireworks. Watch videos Pushpa star Allu Arjun's fans gathered in huge numbers outside his home to wish him on his 44th birthday. The actor stepped out to massive cheers, fireworks and more as he greeted them.

The Devil Wears Prada 2 final trailer: Anne Hathaway, Meryl Streep in new crisis The trailer for The Devil Wears Prada 2 shows Anne Hathaway and Meryl Streep's reunion for a new crisis. The upcoming film brings back the original stars and introduces new faces.

It's actually fun: Harman Baweja on his job as producer for the series Kaptaan Harman Baweja is the writer-producer of the new series, Kaptaan. The actor, who debuted with Love Story 2050 opposite Priyanka Chopra and made a mark with Hansal Mehta's Scoop, opened up about his shift from acting to producing, and also opened up on the importance of work-life balance.

Rajinikanth refuses to comment on Jana Nayagan delay, shares Jailer 2 update Rajinikanth declined to comment on the delay of Vijay's Jana Nayagan due to CBFC issues. He shared updates on Jailer 2 and RKxKH films and urged youngsters to avoid harmful habits and focus on education.

I was cautious, he was fearless: Aditya Dhar credits Mukesh Chhabra for Dhurandhar Aditya Dhar praised Mukesh Chhabra for shaping Dhurandhar: The Revenge with his fearless vision and dedication. He said Chhabra's casting made the film feel more real, adding that the department is often overlooked despite being a crucial part of filmmaking.

Samay Raina breaks down on stage; recalls fear, guilt and chaos after Latent row Comedian Samay Raina recalled the chaos and emotional turmoil following the vandalism at The Habitat after the Ranveer Allahbadia controversy. He also shared how people associated with him were deeply affected due to what transpired after the show was shut down.

Chiraiya writer reacts to backlash, trolling on marital rape theme: Didn't foresee it Chiraiya writer Divy Nidhi Sharma reacted to backlash and trolling over the show's marital rape theme, saying he didn't foresee it. He added that such reactions only highlight why stories like Chiraiya are important.

Zendaya's Euphoria to conclude with Season 3: Check India premiere date and time Euphoria creator Sam Levinson shared his plans for the finale as the final season arrives in India with new dramatic turns. The third season of the show, featuring Zendaya, also has new additions such as Natasha Lyonne and Sharon Stone.

Ram Charan's Peddi likely to miss April release, makers consider June: Reports Ram Charan's much-awaited film Peddi might face another delay, missing its April 30 release date. Reports suggest that the makers are planning to announce a new release date likely after the IPL concludes in June.

Aneet Padda to play double role in Aditya Sarpotdar's Shakti Shalini: Report Actor Aneet Padda is set to take on a double role in Shakti Shalini, which will mark a shift from her previous on-screen image. Directed by Aditya Sarpotdar, the upcoming film is slated for a December release.

Mrunal Thakur dives into action in a saree for Dacoit, calls it toughest genre yet Mrunal Thakur turned action star with Dacoit, calling it her toughest genre yet as she takes on high-octane stunts for the first time. The actor opened up about stepping out of her comfort zone, performing intense sequences, and discovering a new side to her craft.

Atlee's birthday gift to Allu Arjun is Raaka, an idea he held for 18 years Director Atlee shared a special message on Allu Arjun's birthday, while unveiling the title and first-look poster of their film, Raaka. The grand fantasy actioner is expected to release in 2027.

Price of women standing up: Samay Raina on Apoorva Mukhija FIR row Comedian Samay Raina highlighted the courage and cost faced by Apoorva Mukhija after she bravely stood up against derogatory remarks, receiving FIRs and threats in return.

Ranveer Singh is why I exist in films, he's my lucky charm: Mrunal Thakur Mrunal Thakur has expressed heartfelt gratitude towards Ranveer Singh, calling him her lucky charm for shaping her early film career. She also praised Ranveer's deep and layered performance in Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Priyadarshan: Akshay Kumar can look like a common man, SRK is very urban Filmmaker Priyadarshan shared insights on Akshay Kumar and Shah Rukh Khan's acting nuances. In an exclusive interview, he noted that Akshay can look like a common man but Shah Rukh Khan can't, since his body language is very polished and urban.

Raaka: Allu Arjun turns animalistic in Atlee's next, first look poster out On Allu Arjun's 44th birthday, the makers of his upcoming film with Atlee revealed its title, Raaka, along with a striking first poster. Also starring Deepika Padukone, the film marks their first collaboration.

Samay Raina shares the bold Abhishek Bachchan joke he didn't tell Big B on KBC Samay Raina shared behind-the-scenes anecdotes from Kaun Banega Crorepati, including the joke he chose not to make to Amitabh Bachchan and the story he told to "play the room." In his latest special, he also revisited the Ranveer Allahbadia controversy, revealing what really happened off-camera.

Amitabh Bachchan shares cryptic note on life's uncertainties, calls truth elusive Amitabh Bachchan shares a cryptic blog post reflecting on life's uncertainties and the search for meaning. The actor's introspective note highlights the elusive nature of answers and his ongoing journey of self-reflection.
Samay Raina announces India's Got Latent Season 2 a year after controversy Samay Raina announced the return of his popular roast-style talent show, India's Got Latent, with a bolder new season.

general news.headline: Mojtaba supervising everything, his council taking decisions on war: Khamenei's aide to India TodayMojtaba supervising everything, his council taking decisions on war: Khamenei's aide to India Today

Why US-Iran ceasefire is unlikely to last. The reason is in LebanonThe US and Iran announced a two-week ceasefire after exchanging air, missile and drone strikes for a month. The truce is fragile because of the terms of agreements. The biggest test will be in Lebanon, with Israel refusing to stop its combat operations there.

What was Pakistan's role in US-Iran ceasefire? Journalist Hamid Mir explainsPakistani journalist Hamid Mir said Pakistan acted as a facilitator rather than a direct mediator in efforts to secure a two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran.

How 'farcical', 'b***s**t' Israeli intel led Trump to Iran warThe origins of the Iran conflict can be traced back to a closed-door briefing in the White House that was attended by Israeli PM Netanyahu, Trump and his aides. After the meeting, several of Trump's aides, including Marco Rubio and JD Vance, were not on the same page regarding a military campaign in Iran.

I stopped using Blinkit and Instamart for 30 days. My wallet (and health) thanked meI deleted Blinkit and Instamart from my phone just to see if I could survive in this digital-first world. It seems, I thrived.

Oman says no toll on Hormuz crossing, contradicts Iran's truce demandOman said agreements bar charging ships for passing through the Strait of Hormuz. Under international maritime law, countries along the strait cannot levy transit fees but may charge limited service fees, such as for piloting or tugging, applied without discrimination.

Borrowing to survive, facing abuse: Inside India's growing credit stressNearly 4 in 10 borrowers report abusive recovery calls, as a new survey highlights rising repayment stress and growing dependence on credit for everyday needs.

Sir, I'll tell you everything: Constable key to death penalty for 9 Tamil Nadu copsHead Constable Revathi's courageous testimony, defying threats and institutional pressure, proved pivotal in securing death sentences for nine Tamil Nadu police personnel five years after a father and son died in custodial torture.

US-Iran have agreed to a ceasefire, but will it last?Even as the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, sirens blared across Gulf countries for incoming missiles. Besides, Netanyahu and mediator Shehbaz Sharif gave contradictory statements on Lebanon. While the truce has been announced, the big question is: will it last?

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

Live: First ships cross Hormuz as Iran opens waterway after ceasefire agreementUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: In a sudden shift from threats to pause, Donald Trump said he would halt US military action against Iran, at least for now, signalling a possible opening for diplomacy. Posting on Truth Social, Trump said, "I agree to suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for a period of two weeks," framing the move as part of a broader push toward a ceasefire. The decision, he said, followed conversations with Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Army chief Asim Munir, who urged him to hold off on planned strikes. Iran has accepted Pakistan's two-week ceasefire proposal. The ceasefire has been approved by the country's new supreme leader, signalling a possible opening for de-escalation. Iran said talks with the US should not be interpreted as signalling the end of the war, adding that its 10-point proposal calls for lifting all primary and secondary sanctions, full compensation payments, and the release of all frozen Iranian assets. Tehran said it will only accept the war's conclusion once all details are finalised in line with the 10-point plan, Iranian media reported. Negotiations with the US are to be held in Islamabad on 10th April (Friday) to finalise details. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

China, not Pakistan, the real player behind US-Iran ceasefire? Live UpdatesOver the course of the Iran-US war, China, one of Tehran's key allies, remained a silent player. However, it has now emerged that China's discreet diplomacy and a last-minute intervention got Iran on board for the two-week ceasefire deal. Thus, China, and not Pakistan, has emerged as the real player.

Minerva's historic win vs Liverpool: A Spain trip that almost did not happenMinerva Academy kids created history when they beat Liverpool U15s 6-0 in the MIC Cup, a prestigious international youth tournament. Here's the untold story of how the historic trip almost did not happen.

24 hours on, where is Pawan Khera as Assam cops look for the Congress leader?Congress leader Pawan Khera has triggered a major political storm in poll-bound Assam with allegations against the Chief Minister's wife. But where is he now?

TVK loses one seat even before its debut contest in Tamil Nadu pollsThe nomination of a TVK candidate from the Edappadi constituency has been rejected, and the candidate has now gone missing, with the party alleging kidnapping.

India's salary hike projection: Who's winning and who's losing in 2026India's salary hikes have stabilised at 9.1% in 2026, but companies have tightened performance ratings, reshaped promotions, and shifted focus to skills, making career growth more competitive across sectors despite steady pay increments.

5 statements made by Samay Raina on Still Alive that are raw, risky, unmissableWe have compiled comedian Samay Raina's five statements from the show that made the audience laugh, pause, reflect, and, at times, even tear up.

Trump, not Iran, blinked first in the war. Why?US President Donald Trump's war cry turned into just a cry, and then a walk-back. Because at home, there's a war too. A figurative one. His threats to wipe out a civilisation echoed louder in the US than they could in Iran. Here's why Trump blinked, again.

When Sanjeev Kapoor refused to follow dress code to receive Padma Shri, and wonSanjeev Kapoor, one of the most celebrated chefs in the country, had one condition before accepting the Padma Shri, a gesture that honoured his entire community of chefs.

Amazon responds after reports claim it may lay off 14,000 employees next monthAmazon is reportedly planning for another major round of layoffs. The American giant may fire as many as 14,000 workers across divisions globally with some teams possibly shutting down. The company has cut 16,000 roles in January this year.

The Devil Wears Prada 2 final trailer: Anne Hathaway, Meryl Streep in new crisisThe trailer for The Devil Wears Prada 2 shows Anne Hathaway and Meryl Streep's reunion for a new crisis. The upcoming film brings back the original stars and introduces new faces.

Deepika Padukone defends her silence on Dhurandhar 2, trolls the trollDeepika Padukone put an end to the social media gossip about her silence on Ranveer Singh's blockbuster Dhurandhar: The Revenge. The actor gave a witty reply on Instagram, proving support isn't always loud.

Has Trump used Madman Theory in Iran war and ceasefire?In his constant warnings to Iran, US President Donald Trump might be following the Madman Theory that was used by former US President Richard Nixon during the Vietnam War. What is the Madman Theory, and is the Iran war ceasefire a result of that?

Iranian oil heads to India after China detour, ending 7-year import freezeFor the first time in seven years, India is set to receive the first Iranian crude vessel, with Indian Oil Corporation procuring a shipment following US sanctions relief.

sports.headline: CSK CEO updates on MS Dhoni's injury: Will he play team's next match vs DC? Chennai Super Kings will continue to miss MS Dhoni in their next IPL 2026 game. CSK CEO confirmed the news, stating that Dhoni was yet to return to full fitness from his calf injury.

Minerva's historic win vs Liverpool: A Spain trip that almost did not happen Minerva Academy kids created history when they beat Liverpool U15s 6-0 in the MIC Cup, a prestigious international youth tournament. Here's the untold story of how the historic trip almost did not happen.

Is Fleming the problem for CSK? Ex-India star says coach dependent on Dhoni IPL 2026: Stephen Fleming finds himself under pressure as CSK's struggles continue, with questions now being asked about his impact without MS Dhoni leading the side. As losses pile up, the spotlight has firmly shifted from players to the dugout.

Badminton Asia Champs: Lakshya Sen crashes out in first round, Sindhu advances India had a horror start in the Badminton Asia Championships after Lakshya Sen crashed out against Hong Kong China's Lee Cheuk Yiu in straight games. India's challenge in the competition remains alive as Ayush Shetty and PV Sindhu advanced to the next round.

Bayern's 2014 Neuer comes out of syllabus for Real Madrid in UCL defeat Bayern Munich secured a 2–1 win over Real Madrid at the Bernabéu, but it was 40-year-old Manuel Neuer who stole the spotlight with a sensational nine-save performance. Despite Bayern's dominance and missed chances, Madrid remain firmly in the tie ahead of the second leg in Munich.

Beyond cricket's billions: Why hockey's 'El Dorado' lies in speed, not sprawl As the IPL cements its status as a billion-dollar spectacle, Indian hockey finds itself battling irrelevance, caught between nostalgia and a failure to adapt to modern sport's demands. Can hockey make a comeback in an era of constant visibility, monetisation, and reinvention?

When will J&K pacer Auqib Nabi make his IPL debut? IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals are set to play Gujarat Titans at their own backyard on Wednesday, April 8. Despite acquiring Jammu and Kashmir pacer Auqib Nabi for Rs 8.40 crore, Delhi have not handed him a debut yet in the IPL.

DC vs GT: Gill back for struggling Gujarat as Delhi eye a fortress at Kotla IPL, DC vs GT Preview: Delhi Capitals look to build on their strong start at home as they chase a hat-trick of wins. Shubman Gill's return, meanwhile, offers a timely boost to a struggling Gujarat Titans side.

Bindaas Vaibhav Sooryavanshi forcing Jaiswal to level-up at Rajasthan Royals IPL, RR vs MI: Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ripped apart Mumbai Indians in Guwahati on April 7. After the win, Jaiswal spoke about how young Sooryavanshi was forcing him to level up at Rajasthan Royals.

Sooryvanshi's evolution will be fun to watch: MI coach Jayawardane on RR starboy IPL 2026: Mahela Jayawardene praised Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's fearless knock against MI, calling his evolution exciting to watch. The 15-year-old stunned Jasprit Bumrah with two sixes, continuing his formidable start to the season.

IPL Play Of The Day: MI's big guns fail to replicate Jaiswal-Sooryavanshi blitz IPL 2026: On a rainy day which saw the game being reduced to 11 overs, RR sent out Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yashasvi Jaiswal to play a bit of contra on the field and shoot at everything. But when it came to MI, the approach seemed to lack the intent needed to pull off a big chase.

HD Throwback: Why Vaibhav Sooryavanshi vs Bumrah felt like Sachin vs QadirIPL 2026: 37 years after Sachin Tendulkar's sixes against Abdul Qadir in Peshawar, history finds a high-definition mirror in Guwahati. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's fearless takedown of Jasprit Bumrah captured the same rare, transcendent magic as that legendary 1989 encounter on Tuesday.

IPL 2026: How Buttler is trusting his game and experience for GT form revivalJos Buttler is backing his own game and experience to turn things around in the IPL with the Gujarat Titans after a poor 2026 in T20Is. Buttler has had a decent start to his IPL 2026 campaign but is yet to contribute a big total.

Storm after rain: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yashasvi Jaiswal blow MI awayIPL 2026, RR vs MI: Rain couldn't stop the Rajasthan Royals' momentum as Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yashasvi Jaiswal turned an 11-over shootout into a masterclass in carnage. Sinking Mumbai Indians by 27 runs in Guwahati, the 2008 champions remain flawless in IPL 2026, reclaiming the top spot with their third consecutive win.

Candidates chess: Praggnanandhaa loses, almost out of contention for top placeIndian Grandmaster R Praggnanandhaa suffered a setback in the Candidates tournament after losing to Anish Giri in the eighth round in Chennai. The loss has hurt his chances of finishing at the top.

Ravi Shastri stand to be unveiled at Wankhede, MCA to honour Mumbai greatsThe Mumbai cricket association has decided to unveil the Ravi Shastri stand at the Wankhede Stadium on Thursday, April 8. Along with Shastri, Diana Edulji, Dilip Sardesai will also be honoured by the MCA.

Mohammed Shami should play for India again: Ganguly sends reminder to AgarkarIPL 2026: Former India captain Sourav Ganguly has firmly backed Mohammed Shami's return to the senior national team, stating that the 35-year-old pacer remains supremely fit and hungry to keep adding to his wicket tally. Shami has been in impressive form for Lucknow Super Giants in the ongoing IPL season.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stuns Bumrah with first-ball six, MI ace can only smileVaibhav Sooryavanshi showed no fear against Jasprit Bumrah and dispatched the Indian star pacer for a first-ball six in Guwahati.

After PSL ball-tampering scandal, Fakhar Zaman addresses T20I retirement rumoursPakistan opener Fakhar Zaman has dismissed speculation around his T20I retirement, clarifying that he has no immediate plans to step away from the shortest format despite recent controversy during the Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2026 season.

Billie Jean King Cup: Unforced errors haunt Vaishnavi, India trail 0-1 vs ThailandIndia's Vaishnavi Adkar suffered a straight-sets defeat to Thailand's Aunchisa Chanta as the hosts went down 0-1 in their opening Asia/Oceania Group I tie of the Billie Jean King Cup on Tuesday.

How RCB homecoming changed Padikkal and helped him find his brand of cricketDevdutt Padikkal revealed how the IPL 2025 auction was the turning point of his career and how he found his style of cricket during his second stint in RCB. Padikkal has scored two fifties in as many games during IPL 2026.

High-flying DC not feeling Starc's absence yet: Bowling coach Munaf PatelDelhi Capitals bowling coach Munaf Patel has said that the team isn't Mitchell Starc just yet after winning their first two games of the IPL 2026 season. Starc is currently recovering from an injury.

Gujarat Titans receive timely Shubman Gill boost before Delhi Capitals gameIPL 2026, DC vs GT: Sai Sudharsan has confirmed that Shubman Gill will return to lead the Gujarat Titans in their IPL 2026 clash against Delhi Capitals at the Arun Jaitley Stadium on Wednesday.

Ex-Arsenal and Juventus midfielder Aaron Ramsey announces retirementAaron Ramsey has announced his immediate retirement from professional football, bringing an end to a 20-year career that saw him represent top clubs including Arsenal and Juventus. The Welsh midfielder also played a key role for the Wales national football team, notably helping them reach the semi-finals of UEFA Euro 2016 and qualify for the FIFA World Cup 2022.

Tamim replaces Aminul Islam as Bangladesh cricket chief after T20 World Cup rowTamim Iqbal takes charge as the new Bangladesh cricket president, replacing Aminul Islam as the country looks to rebuild following the T20 World Cup fiasco.

Will Sooryavanshi treat Bumrah like any other bowler? Pundits predictVaibhav Sooryavanshi and Jasprit Bumrah are all set to battle it out in a prodigy vs master clash when RR face MI in Guwahati on Tuesday, April 8. Aaron Finch and Abhinav Mukund predict how the battle could unfold between the two stars.

RR vs MI Highlights: Rajasthan crush Mumbai in rain-curtailed Guwahati clashRajasthan Royals (RR) vs Mumbai Indians (MI) Highlights, IPL 2026: Rajasthan Royals surged to three wins in three, extending their flawless start with a convincing 27-run victory in a rain-shortened 11-over clash. The win takes RR to 3-0 in IPL 2026, while Mumbai Indians, after beginning with a victory, have now stumbled to back-to-back defeats.

Harry Maguire signs contract extension at Manchester UnitedFormer captain Harry Maguire has signed a new one-year contract with Manchester United, with the option to extend for a further year. The 33-year-old central defender had been nearing the end of his current deal, which would expire at the conclusion of the season.

Real Madrid vs Bayern Munich: European Clasico returns, odds tilt German wayBayern Munich arrive in Madrid as the more settled side, while Real Madrid rely on big-game moments. As the European Clasico returns, form slightly favours the German champions heading into a high-stakes Champions League quarter-final clash.

Warner arrested for drink-driving in Sydney. Will it affect Pak league stint?Former Australian opener David Warner was arrested for drink-driving in Sydney on Sunday while on a break from his Pakistan Super League duties. Warner has been leading the Karachi Kings in the 11th edition of the PSL.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
